items.md: # 5 - November 1968 - 196 pages
items.value: 5 - November 1968 - 196 pages

items.md:
DONALD DUCK AND UNCLE SCROOGE The Strange Shipwrecks - 21 (pp. 3-12, 99-109) - A reprint of "UNCLE SCROOGE and The Strange Shipwrecks" in *Uncle Scrooge* No. 23, Sept.-Nov. 1958.
*Changes:* Title changed as indicated.
DAISY DUCK'S DIARY The Dainty Daredevil - 8 (pp. 67-74) - art only - Daisy, trying to find a job, is misled by a fortune teller and takes a job as a stunt girl for a daredevil pilot. (June 18, 1968)
These pages are two panels deep.
Barks: "The Daisy Duck story in D.C. Digest must be one I drew for Chase \[Craig] last June. 8 pages. He was desperate for someone to do the art. All the current ink slingers having been busy or away on vacation." (Nov. 22, 1968 letter to Michael Barrier)
items.value:
DONALD DUCK AND UNCLE SCROOGE The Strange Shipwrecks - 21 (pp. 3-12, 99-109) - A reprint of "UNCLE SCROOGE and The Strange Shipwrecks" in Uncle Scrooge No. 23, Sept.-Nov. 1958.
Changes: Title changed as indicated.
DAISY DUCK'S DIARY The Dainty Daredevil - 8 (pp. 67-74) - art only - Daisy, trying to find a job, is misled by a fortune teller and takes a job as a stunt girl for a daredevil pilot. (June 18, 1968)
These pages are two panels deep.
Barks: "The Daisy Duck story in D.C. Digest must be one I drew for Chase [Craig] last June. 8 pages. He was desperate for someone to do the art. All the current ink slingers having been busy or away on vacation." (Nov. 22, 1968 letter to Michael Barrier)

items.md:
DONALD DUCK Mystery of the Loch - 13 (originally 10) (pp. 3-15) - Reprinted from *Walt Disney's Comics* No. 237, June 1960.
*Changes:* The panels of the original ten-page story were trimmed and partially redrawn so that the pages could be made three panels deep. The eighth panel on the third page of the original story was deleted from the reprint.
